sources_exist.note: Of 13 URL-bearing citation occurrences, only the McKinsey ownership-transition page resolved to accessible supporting content. The Hinge PDF, Source Global, Bain, BCG, WSJ, Hinge landing-page, and Equiteq URLs were inaccessible or could not be found at the cited paths; repeated occurrences were audited separately.

figures_match_sources.note: No cited URL directly supports its full score-affecting claim. The sole resolving McKinsey page supports national facts that more than half of US small-business owners are over 55 and one in four is 65+, but not a 38% age-60+ rate for management-consulting owners, the quoted less-than-30% next-generation-transfer statistic, or a consulting-specific shortage.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The declared fractions and pi values are bounded and have concrete prose rationales, but their critical empirical premises are unauditable: material adoption=30%, an 8-10-year software analog, effort-based billing shares, owners_60plus_pct=0.38, eight qualifying PE-backed consolidators, and the 4.5x/9.0x multiple endpoints. t50_years=4.0 is therefore not a source-grounded derivation, and pi_dist/pi_moat cannot be accepted against the cited evidence.

rubric_consistent.note: Formula structure, no-proxy separation, and heterogeneous=true follow the frozen rubric, but B uses an unsupported consulting owner estimate, an unsupported succession bonus, and an eight-platform list that includes entities not shown to be PE-backed qualifying add-on platforms. M uses inaccessible Equiteq citations and unsupported normalized paid multiples. The Stage-4 BORDERLINE flag is a review trigger rather than an error, and the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag on S is non-blocking; neither cures these rubric failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift and terminal-value discussions are candid and the no-proxy structure is correct. However, the no-proxy response attributes A to a Hinge technology-adoption survey that the cited URL does not resolve to, while pricing-model deflation presents a precise 65%/18%/12%/5% billing mix as fact despite no accessible cited source supporting it. Those unsupported statements feed C and A.

source_audits.note: The cited PDF path was inaccessible and no matching Hinge publication was findable at that URL. It cannot support boutique client ownership, effort-anchored pricing, or AI-value pass-through.
The specific Source Global report path was inaccessible and was not findable as the cited publication. It cannot support pi_dist=0.38 or the claimed boutique-pricing mechanism.

The cited Bain URL was inaccessible and search surfaced different Bain AI/workforce publications, not this title or path. No accessible content at the citation supports the claimed boutique consulting capture logic.
This repeated citation occurrence was audited separately. The PDF path was inaccessible and cannot support the claimed AI-bypass threat, client insourcing, or pi_moat=0.35.
The cited BCG press URL was inaccessible and search returned other BCG AI publications, not this item. It provides no auditable support for boutique-management-consulting substitution barriers.
The cited WSJ article path was inaccessible and no matching article was findable. It cannot support the claimed current disruption of boutique consulting or pi_moat=0.35.

The cited Hinge landing-page path was inaccessible; findable High Growth Study pages use different URLs and discuss growth/marketing samples, not the quoted 65% AI-tool-use statistic or the estimated 30% material adoption for sub-$25M consulting firms.

This repeated citation occurrence was audited separately. The path was inaccessible and no accessible Hinge study found at it establishes 8-10-year diffusion cycles for CRM, analytics, project management, internet, or content-marketing technology.

The McKinsey page resolves and reports that more than half of US small-business owners are over 55 and one in four is 65+, with 127,000 projected exits in professional, scientific, and technical services. It does not report a 38% age-60+ rate for management-consulting owners, a consulting-sector adjustment, or the quoted claim that fewer than 30% of family businesses transfer to the next generation.

The Equiteq M&A path was inaccessible and not findable as cited. It cannot support the 55-65% PE transaction share, the named-platform statuses and dates, or the count of eight qualifying PE-backed 541611 consolidators.

The Equiteq valuation path was inaccessible and not findable as cited. It provides no auditable support for 4x-5x small-firm transactions, a 4.3x GF Data median, the stored 4.5x cash-at-close value, or market-rate-owner-comp normalization.

This repeated citation occurrence was audited separately. The inaccessible Equiteq path cannot support 8x-10x platform transactions, a 9.1x GF Data median, or the stored 9.0x exit multiple for $10M+ EBITDA consulting platforms.

The generic Source Global reports URL was inaccessible and no cited report page was available. It cannot support the exact 60-70% effort-anchored, 15-20% retainer, or 10-15% value/outcome pricing shares, nor the quoted below-20% outcome-pricing market share.

reasons: $.inputs.pi_dist.sources cites inaccessible URLs at https://hinge.pub/hubfs/research/technology-report-2024.pdf, https://www.source.global/reports/consulting-ai-2024, and https://www.bain.com/insights/for-consultants-navigating-the-future-of-work/. Re-run with accessible, directly relevant boutique-tier evidence for client ownership, billing structure, and observed AI-driven repricing before declaring pi_dist.

$.inputs.pi_moat.sources cites inaccessible URLs at https://hinge.pub/hubfs/research/technology-report-2024.pdf, https://www.bcg.com/press/20october2023-bcg-ai-transformation-client-delivery, and https://www.wsj.com/articles/ai-consulting-disruption-boutique-firms-2024. Re-run with accessible evidence scoped to management-consulting substitution barriers and client AI insourcing before declaring pi_moat.

$.inputs.current_adoption_pct.value=0.30 and $.inputs.t50_years.value=4.0 are not supported by https://hingemarketing.com/library/story/high-growth-study-professional-services, which is inaccessible and was not findable as a study reporting 65% tool use or 30% material boutique adoption. Re-run with a directly accessible adoption survey whose population and material-use definition match 541611 and the in-band tier.

$.inputs.historical_analogs claims 8-10-year technology diffusion cycles that are not supported by https://hingemarketing.com/library/story/high-growth-study-professional-services. Re-run with traceable historical adoption data for comparable consulting technologies and show how it maps to the t50 derivation.

$.inputs.owners_60plus_pct.value=0.38 and $.inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented.value=true exceed what https://www.mckinsey.com/institute-for-economic-mobility/our-insights/the-great-ownership-transfer-a-new-era-of-business-stewardship establishes: it gives broad US small-business age facts, not a consulting age-60+ rate or the quoted next-generation-transfer statistic. Re-run with consulting-specific age and succession evidence, or label and use only a rubric-valid fallback without an unsupported bonus.

$.inputs.active_consolidators.value=8 is unsupported by the inaccessible https://www.equiteq.com/insights/consulting-ma-activity, and the prose list does not demonstrate that every named entity is a PE-backed platform with a qualifying 541611 add-on in the last 24 months. Re-run with platform-by-platform sponsor, deal, subsegment, and date evidence.

$.inputs.buy_mult.value=4.5 and $.inputs.exit_mult.value=9.0 are unsupported by the inaccessible repeated citation https://www.equiteq.com/insights/consulting-firm-valuation. Re-run with accessible transaction evidence, separate small-target and $10M+ platform scopes, adjust earnouts consistently, and document market-rate owner/partner compensation normalization.

$.inputs.pricing_structure and $.cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation state precise billing shares that https://www.source.global/reports does not make auditable. Re-run with a direct report or primary survey establishing the boutique-tier revenue mix and use the same supported mix in pi_dist and the cross-check.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on S
